mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate cut trend is sustained rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, the market prices persistent policy restraint and weaker valuation multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes generate positive 5-day SPX returns more than 40% of the time, or if the sign of the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is above its recent trend.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above 4.8% signals labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if the signal is not negative after accounting for prior PAYEMS trends.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand growth that supports earnings expectations, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days should be positive.
failure_condition: Reject if joint demand-strength episodes fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus either series alone over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO while CPIAUCSL remains firm indicates supply-side stagnation with sticky inflation, which compresses margins and should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not show negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the effect reverses when CPIAUCSL is softening.

mechanism_narrative: A rising HOUST series reflects stronger residential demand and wealth effects, which improve cyclical growth expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising housing starts fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns over baseline, or if the rolling 3-year correlation is not positive and stable.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor income and confidence improve simultaneously, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days should be positive.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if same-direction employment improvements do not generate positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if either series alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a policy-tightening regime, a stable CPIAUCSL combined with a high FEDFUNDS rate indicates restrictive real financing conditions that should keep S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, low-inflation periods do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the sign becomes positive when FEDFUNDS remains elevated.
